title: fix: validate reference image downloads
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Returns validation-classified 400 errors when a user-supplied image body disconnects during download.
- Rejects successful responses with unrecognized image bytes instead of relabeling them as JPEG.
- Classifies Replicate `cannot identify image file` prediction failures as user-input errors.
- Adds downloader and Replicate regression coverage and corrects the Seedream reference-image fixture.

Root cause: malformed or interrupted reference-image downloads escaped as 500s, so repeated requests from individual callers made `wan-fast` and `wan-image-pro` appear broadly degraded in the model monitor.

Impact: invalid or unavailable reference images now produce actionable 400 responses and no longer count as model availability failures. Provider routing and successful image handling are unchanged.

Checks:
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npx biome check --write ...`
- `npx vitest run test/image` — 157 passed

gist.headline: Bad pixels, better answers
gist.blurb: Reference images now get checked at the garden gate. Polli will tell you when an upload is broken instead of blaming the whole flowerbed.
gist.summary: Reference-image downloads now validate their bytes and classify interrupted downloads or Replicate "cannot identify image file" failures as actionable 400 input errors instead of 500s.
gist.impact: People using wan-fast and wan-image-pro with unavailable or malformed reference images will receive a clear client error, while model availability monitoring stays focused on actual provider failures.
gist.visual_concept: Polli is a careful image gardener inspecting a delivered reference-picture parcel with a little magnifying glass, separating a torn or scrambled pixel image from healthy image seedlings. A friendly CRT monitor robot can display a clear "400" notice, while code-plants and intact framed pixel landscapes show that the generation garden is still healthy.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny inspector cap, examining a torn pixel-image parcel with a magnifying glass beside a healthy garden of framed reference images and code-plants; a cute CRT monitor robot displays a simple pixel-font "400" while intact image flowers bloom nearby. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender-to-warm-cream gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, nostalgic but beautiful.
